refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combines multiple cycle phases (load, travel, dump) into one requirement, which implies multiple obligations. To be atomic and solution-neutral, it is necessary to separate each phase into its own FR, focusing on what the system must achieve without specifying how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, ensuring the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) is less than or equal to the target.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel cycle.
The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load cycle sequencing and timing
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback
Design load cycle with fixed cycle time parameters meeting target
Incorporate buffer or slack time to ensure cycle time target adherence
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback

coupling_check.notes: Adaptive control logically decouples cycle time achievement from fixed parameters, allowing independent adjustment to meet target under varying conditions.

assumptions_risks: Real-time feedback data is accurate and timely
Control algorithms can respond quickly enough to influence cycle time
Operational policies allow adaptive control adjustments
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including real-time monitoring and control feedback in the FR to clarify the need for adaptive mechanisms supporting cycle time targets.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for adaptive cycle time control, integrating real-time feedback processing and control algorithms.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time feedback data from sensors and subsystems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends control commands to execute adaptive cycle timing adjustments.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication with external systems for feedback and control coordination.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive cycle time control is a core function of NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as most feedback and control logic resides here.
Partial containment possible but would require spreading control logic across vehicle subsystems, increasing diffusion.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control rather than adaptive cycle timing.
Safety Services may monitor but not implement adaptive cycle control, leading to diffusion if contained here.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the adaptive cycle time control DP, as it centralizes the control logic and real-time feedback processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle subsystems for sensor data may cause some coupling outside NGHE Solution.
If real-time feedback interfaces are not well defined, control logic may leak into other modules.
Changes in feedback mechanisms might require interface updates, risking diffusion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider refining the DP to explicitly include interface definitions for feedback data acquisition and control command dispatch to better encapsulate dependencies within NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback' directly addresses the FR of supporting achievement of the customer-defined cycle time target by adapting control based on feedback, which aligns with the acceptance criterion of measured cycle time p95 ≤ CT_target.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle subsystems for sensor data may cause some coupling outside NGHE Solution.
If real-time feedback interfaces are not well defined, control logic may leak into other modules.
Changes in feedback mechanisms might require interface updates, risking diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion with a containment score of 8 and diffusion score of 2, indicating that the DP is well contained within this IM boundary. Other modules show lower containment and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the acceptance criterion specifies measurable cycle time performance (p95 ≤ CT_target) under defined duty cycle and site conditions, which can be tested through system-level performance evaluation of the NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, as this module encompasses the core system logic responsible for adaptive cycle time control and integrates real-time feedback processing and control algorithms, matching the FR intent.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly include interface definitions for feedback data acquisition and control command dispatch to better encapsulate dependencies within the NGHE Solution module, reducing diffusion risk.
Monitor and manage dependencies on Vehicle subsystems to minimize coupling and potential diffusion of control logic.
Ensure that real-time feedback interfaces are well defined and stable to prevent leakage of control logic into other modules.

decision.justification: The current FR and DP are well aligned and the DP is well contained within the NGHE Solution IM, with verification feasibility and clear allocation. However, given the high safety criticality and identified diffusion risks related to dependencies on Vehicle subsystems and real-time feedback interfaces, it is prudent to decompose the FR further into sub-FRs that explicitly address interface definitions, data acquisition, and control command dispatch. This will help to better encapsulate dependencies, reduce diffusion risk, and improve architectural clarity and safety assurance.
next_level_sub_frs: Define interface for real-time feedback data acquisition
Implement adaptive control logic for cycle time adjustment
Define interface for control command dispatch to actuators

revise.why: Refine the DP to explicitly include interface definitions for feedback data acquisition and control command dispatch to better encapsulate dependencies within the NGHE Solution module, reducing diffusion risk and improving safety assurance.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary implementation boundary for adaptive cycle time control.
Real-time feedback data and control commands are critical interfaces that impact system safety and performance.
open_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle subsystems causing coupling outside NGHE Solution.
Risk of control logic leakage due to poorly defined interfaces.
Changes in feedback mechanisms requiring interface updates and risking diffusion.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface specifications for feedback data acquisition and control command dispatch.
Verification test results demonstrating cycle time performance under defined conditions.
Architectural analysis showing containment and diffusion metrics after refinement.
